course_description:
The purpose of the professional doctorate in psychology is to provide advanced postgraduate research training in Clinical Psychology, Clinical Neuropsychology and Clinical Psychology or Clinical Psychology and Clinical Geropsychology.

The research component of the Doctor of Psychology program aims to foster the development of independent research skills that make a substantial contribution to the discipline, which will be demonstrated by the submission of a research dissertation.

The dissertation entails enrolment in a dissertation course over 6 semesters.ﾠ Students will enrol in a combination of either PYSC8005 (4 units) or PSYC8010 (8 units) over 6 semesters.ﾠ The standard sequence is for students to enrol in a combination of PSYC8005 Dissertation - In progressﾠand PSYC8010 Dissertation - In progress over 5 semesters, with enrolment in PSYC8005 Dissertation in the 6th semester when thesis submission will occur.ﾠ

The dissertation should be of sufficient depth and scope to reflect the research skills that are described by the Australian Qualification Framework as being necessary for a program at a Level 10 Doctoral Degree:

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
Registration with Psychology Board of Australia
incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

course_aims: The course aims to foster the development of independent research skills that make a substantial contribution to the discipline, which will be demonstrated by the submission of a research dissertation. The dissertation willﾠprovide a contribution to knowledge with a level of originality consistent with a research higher degree.ﾠIt should also reveal a capacity to relate the research topic to the broader framework of knowledge in the disciplinary area integrating a practice based model.ﾠThe dissertation will be on a topic that is negotiated with the supervisor and approved by the Director of Clinical Programs.ﾠ The dissertation should have a clinical psychology, clinical psychology andﾠgeropsychology,ﾠor clinical neuropsychology and clinical psychologyﾠorientation depending on the particular stream undertaken.

learning_outcomes.description: Evaluate, critically review, and synthesise the literature and knowledge base in the field of study
Design and implement a research project with the guidance of a supervisor
Use research methodologies to extend and redefine existing knowledge or professional practice
Communicate the results of the study effectively through the preparation and submission of the written thesis
Disseminate and promote new insights to peers and the community
Design, conduct, and report research in a manner consistent with relevant ethical guidelines

assessment_details.title: DPsych Dissertation
assessment_details.due_date: The dissertation is due for submission in the 6th semester of consecutive enrolment
assessment_details.task_description: The Dissertation counts for half of the course (32 out of 64 units). The dissertation should be of sufficient depth and scope to reflect the research skills that are described by the Australian Qualification Framework as being necessary for a program at a Level 10 Doctoral Degree. The dissertation can take the form of a traditional research-only thesis, or it can be in the form of a substantial paper or series of papers (ready for submission or submitted), with a general Introduction and a unifying Discussion. The form of the dissertation should be discussed and agreed with the supervisor. The usual minimum length of the dissertation is 30,000 words and the maximum length should not exceed 50,000 words, excluding Tables, Figures, References, and Appendices. The writing must conform to the most current American Psychological Association style requirements. The dissertation will be submitted when enrolled in PSYC8005/PSYC8010 Dissertation and is the formal assessment of 6 semesters of enrolment in the dissertation courses and should demonstrate the reporting of a substantial piece of original research that has been conducted by the candidate for the prescribed period under proper academic supervision in a research environment. By the end of the DP candidature, the candidate should be capable of independently conceiving, designing, carrying out and reporting a major research project. Further information on the Dissertation courses including dissertation preparation and submission requirements can be found on the Blackboard Community [Doctor of Psychology Dissertation] website. assessment_details.title: Confirmation Milestone (completed Yr2,Sem 2)
assessment_details.due_date: The confirmation milestone must be completed by the last day of teaching (Wk 13) in the semester.
assessment_details.task_description: The Confirmation Milestone is undertaken in Semester 2 of Year 2 of standard enrolment in the Dissertation course. It consists of the following: 1) preparation of a 10 pp. (excluding references and appendices) document, uploaded into the Turnitin Folder of the Blackboard Community website [Doctor of Psychology Dissertation]. 2) a 20-minute presentation of your research plan and progress to date, with 10 minutes for questions, followed by a 30-minute interview with the course coordinator, supervisor and reader. The confirmation document will have two readers: the Course Coordinator and a second reader who will be approached by the supervisor for his/her agreement to review the document and their name provided with the confirmation document when it is submitted. The confirmation document will need to be uploaded 2 weeks before the scheduled interview times to allow the coordinator and readers time to review. After completing the different components of the milestone process, a report will be sent by the Course Coordinator to the supervisor for signature by him/her and the student. The completed report is to be uploaded by the student through Turnitin in the Assessment Folder on the Blackboard site and the Course Coordinator will approve the form AFTER uploading. assessment_details.title: Mid-Dissertation Milestone (completed Yr3,Sem 2)
assessment_details.due_date: The mid-dissert milestone must be completed by the last day of teaching (wk 13) in the semester.
assessment_details.task_description: The mid-dissertation review is undertaken in Semester 2 of Year 3 of standard enrolment in the Dissertation course. There are three components to the mid-dissertation review: • written work; • a brief report summarising progress against the timeline agreed to at confirmation, and set of goals and plan of work for the period leading up to dissertation review; and • an interview. The specific requirements for the written work will vary on a case by case basis, depending on the nature of the project. Each student should negotiate the requirements with the advisory team and the Course Coordinator (if further advice is needed). For example, the written work might take the form of a systematic literature review, study protocol, methods section, data analytic strategy, or a draft manuscript for publication. Whatever the precise form of the written work it must demonstrate production of work at a level consistent with that expected of a Level 10 degree, including demonstrated knowledge of relevant background research, critical analysis of evidence, and clear exposition of research conducted by the student. The panel will assess whether: • Satisfactory progress has been made since confirmation; • The project remains suitable for a Level 10 dissertation; • The student is capable of completing DPsych level work; • The dissertation promises to be completed within the expected period of enrolment within existing resources; • There are any factors delaying progress; and • The advisory relationship is adequate and effective. This assessment will be made on the basis of the written work and the interview After completing the different components of the milestone process, a report will be sent by the Course Coordinator to the supervisor and student for their signatures. The completed report is to be uploaded by the student to Turnitin in the Assessment Folder on the Blackboard site and the Course Coordinator will approve the form AFTER uploading. learning_activities.topic: Dissertation The candidate will design and plan the research and its timelines, and undertake the research with the guidance of a supervisor, in consideration of the timing of the relevant milestone and submission Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Fitness to Practise Policy School guidelines Your school has additional guidelines you'll need to follow for this course: School of Psychology Student Guidelines Learning activities
